goal: Add a --dry-run flag to scripts/persistent-solve.py that only runs recursive planning and prints the kanban tree, without executing any tasks. This tests the recursive decomposition and kanban output.

tree.description: Add --dry-run argument to the argparse CLI in main() and pass it through to persistent_solve()
Implement dry-run logic in _run_dag_mode: after recursive_plan/plan_dag, build KanbanState, print the tree, write kanban JSON, then return early without executing tasks

children.description: Add --dry-run argparse flag to main() and propagate it through persistent_solve() to _run_dag_mode()
Implement dry-run early-return logic in _run_dag_mode after Phase 1 planning: initialize KanbanState, update from DAG, print tree, save kanban JSON, then return
Add dry_run: bool = False parameter to _run_dag_mode signature and wire it from the CLI argparse
When dry_run=True, force KanbanState creation and kanban_out defaulting to WIP_DIR/kanban.json even if kanban=False
Insert early-return block after Phase 1 planning: call update_from_dag, save, print_tree in order, then return — skipping Phase 2 execution entirely
Ensure the existing 'No tasks generated' print + break still fires before the dry-run return path when dag.tasks is empty
Manually verify dry-run logic by reading the final code path to confirm contract constraints: call order, no execute_* calls, single loop iteration, normal return
Add dry-run status to the banner/header output in persistent_solve so the user sees confirmation that dry-run mode is active
Verify dry-run integration: ensure the for-loop breaks after one iteration, kanban JSON file is written correctly, and no execution functions are reachable

tree.description: Update the module docstring and Usage section to document the --dry-run flag
Manual verification: run the script with --dry-run --recursive and confirm it prints a kanban tree and exits without launching any claude sessions for execution
Commit and push the changes to origin/master
